slides.title: The hours that *steady your mood*
slides.sub: REM sleep is concentrated in the last stretch of the night. It quietly tunes your emotions and your brain.

slides.title: REM does the night shift
slides.body: Dreaming sleep clusters in the *final hours* before you wake. Cut the night short and that is the part you lose first.

slides.title: It sorts your emotions
slides.body: REM helps the brain file emotional memories, so yesterday's stress *feels softer* by morning instead of sharp.

slides.title: Stress hormones settle
slides.body: Overnight, REM is linked to *lower stress chemistry*, giving the brain a calmer window to recover and reset.

slides.title: Mood and a sharper mind
slides.body: Protecting REM can support steadier mood and *healthier brain aging*, two things that compound over decades.

slides.title: Protect the last hours
slides.body: A consistent wake time and a *full night* guard your REM. Cutting sleep short skims it off the top.

slides.title: See your nights *add up*
slides.body: Vibe Health tracks your sleep alongside food, movement and steps, turning the habits that protect REM into one daily Health Score.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
The last hours of your night do the quiet work of steadying your mood. 🌙😌

REM sleep sorts emotional memories and lets overnight stress chemistry settle, protecting your mood and your aging brain. Cut sleep short and it is the first thing you lose. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health turns your sleep and daily habits into one simple score → link in bio.
youtube_title: Why the Last Hours of Sleep Protect Your Mood
